objective: Use the imported project corpus to identify project type, infer item systems from drawings, and produce UAE-ready specification output.

specificationOutputFormats.title: Part 1 - General
specificationOutputFormats.description: Summary of scope, references, submittals, QA/QC, mockups, tolerances, storage, and execution prerequisites.

specificationOutputFormats.title: Part 2 - Products
specificationOutputFormats.description: Material system, approved manufacturers, performance criteria, samples, finishes, and compliance references.

specificationOutputFormats.title: Part 3 - Execution
specificationOutputFormats.description: Preparation, installation, workmanship, testing, protection, and close-out requirements.

specificationOutputFormats.title: Traceability Layer
specificationOutputFormats.description: Each specification item should link to regulation references, source documents, and measurement rules.

trainingRules: Treat uploaded drawings, schedules, callouts, and notes as valid primary evidence.
Use local UAE and Dubai regulation packs first, then match project corpus patterns by type and location.
When evidence is weak, flag assumptions and missing references instead of inventing clauses.
Always connect each specification item to a measurable unit and a traceable source.
